reason: The delivery-delay-batching rule (rules/shared/delivery/delivery-delay-batching.md) already fully covers every API surface introduced by the v5.0.1-beta.4 notification delay and batching pipeline. All four config types (NotificationServiceConfig, DelayConfig, BatchConfig, BatchWindowConfig), all field references, the pipeline chain (delay → seen check → batch → deliver), the webhook exclusion note, and the document/user batching independence constraint are documented correctly and completely. The Agent-8 patch log for the v5.0.1-beta.4 Comments run confirms the notifications library was regenerated with no changes (11 rules). No new rule creation or existing rule update is warranted.

itemsEvaluated.text: Added opt-in notification delay and batching pipeline for comment notifications. Organizations can configure delayConfig and batchConfig in workspace's notificationServiceConfig.
itemsEvaluated.rationale: The delivery-delay-batching.md rule already documents notificationServiceConfig with its delayConfig and batchConfig sub-objects. The rule's correct code example and field reference table cover every property introduced in this release. No update needed.

itemsEvaluated.text: New type: NotificationServiceConfig (top-level container with delayConfig: DelayConfig and batchConfig: BatchConfig).
itemsEvaluated.rationale: The existing rule references notificationServiceConfig as the top-level key and documents delayConfig and batchConfig as its children. The type shape is fully represented in the rule's JSON example and field reference table.
Both fields (delayConfig.isEnabled: boolean and delayConfig.delaySeconds: number) are present in the existing rule's field reference table with correct descriptions.

itemsEvaluated.text: New type: BatchConfig (document: BatchWindowConfig, user: BatchWindowConfig).
itemsEvaluated.rationale: batchConfig.document and batchConfig.user are both present in the existing rule's field reference table and JSON example.
All three fields (isEnabled, batchWindowSeconds, maxActivities) are documented in the existing rule's field reference table for both document and user batching scopes.

itemsEvaluated.text: Document-level and user-level batching operate independently.
itemsEvaluated.rationale: The existing rule explicitly states: 'Each stage is independent: you can enable delay only, batching only, or both. Document-level and user-level batching also operate independently of each other.' No update needed.

itemsEvaluated.text: Webhooks and workflow triggers always fire immediately.
itemsEvaluated.rationale: The existing rule uses the exact canonical phrasing from the QA-aligned docs: 'Webhooks and workflow triggers always fire immediately, regardless of any delay or batch configuration.' The QA note about aligning to "are never subject to delay or batching" phrasing was a changelog-level fix; the rule's phrasing is already semantically correct and complete. No update needed.

itemsEvaluated.text: Pipeline chain: delay → seen check → batch → deliver.
itemsEvaluated.rationale: The existing rule's Pipeline Order section documents all four stages in the correct sequence with descriptions matching the release note and the QA-verified documentation.

itemsEvaluated.text: New documentation section in customize-behavior.mdx: Notification Delivery section with notificationServiceConfig configuration.
itemsEvaluated.rationale: Documentation additions to product docs are not themselves skill deltas. The skill rule already captures all the API surface and behavior described in that section. No new rule or rule update is needed.
